sourceMovie.title: As Good as It Gets
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit As Good as It Gets for the same reason it broke through in 1997: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. James L. Brooks keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Forrest Gump
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If As Good as It Gets clicked for you, Forrest Gump (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after As Good as It Gets?
faq.answer: Try Forrest Gump, Breakfast at Tiffany's, Before We Go—each shares DNA with As Good as It Gets while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is As Good as It Gets?
faq.answer: As Good as It Gets (1997) is best described as Drama, Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is As Good as It Gets worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
